title: China: Huadian Launches 3.9B Yuan Wind-To-Hydrogen Green Methanol Project In Liaoning
content:
On September 21, a groundbreaking ceremony was held for the Huadian Tiaobingshan Wind Power-to-Hydrogen Coupled Green Methanol Demonstration Project in Tiaobingshan, Liaoning. Representing a total investment of 3.945 billion yuan, this initiative is considered a landmark for Huadian Liaoning Company and a pivotal component of the provinceâ€™s revitalization efforts.

Selected as one of the first national pilot projects for green liquid fuel technology, the facility will integrate a 450 MW wind farm with a hydrogen production and storage facility and a 100,000-tonne-per-year methanol synthesis plant. Upon completion, it is projected to produce 19,000 tonnes of green hydrogen and 100,000 tonnes of green methanol annually, establishing an integrated "electricity-hydrogen-chemical industry."

Representatives from the municipal government and China Huadian highlighted the project's significance. Li Wenbiao, speaking for the government, called it a "vivid practice" for fostering new productive forces and Tieling's industrial transformation. Jiang Qingsong of China Huadian emphasized the company's commitment to building a green energy industrial chain in Liaoning. The project is expected to drive economic development by attracting new manufacturing enterprises.
author: Analysts Account
